metadata.description: Pan Teparak's self-authored development skills, one plugin per skill for fine-grained per-repo enablement.

plugins.name: debug-backend
plugins.description: Systematically debug backend issues in any language using structured root cause analysis. Use when investigating bugs, errors, or unexpected behavior in backend services.

plugins.name: debug-db
plugins.description: Debug database issues including slow queries, deadlocks, migration failures, and data integrity problems. Use when troubleshooting database-related issues.

plugins.name: debug-k8s
plugins.description: Debug Kubernetes issues including pod failures, networking, RBAC, and operator problems. Use when troubleshooting K8s deployments or operators.

plugins.name: design-system
plugins.description: Create or audit a design system with consistent components, tokens, and patterns for web/mobile applications. Use when building or reviewing component libraries.

plugins.name: dev-api
plugins.description: Design and implement REST APIs using OpenAPI-first approach with proper versioning, error handling, and documentation. Use when designing or implementing API endpoints.

plugins.name: dev-ci-github
plugins.description: Create GitHub Actions CI/CD workflows for testing, linting, building, and deploying applications. Use when setting up or improving CI/CD pipelines.

plugins.name: dev-ci-gitlab
plugins.description: Create GitLab CI/CD pipelines for testing, linting, building, and deploying applications. Use when setting up or improving GitLab CI pipelines.

plugins.name: dev-django
plugins.description: Develop features in Django + DRF projects using services/selectors pattern, TDD, and sub-app architecture. Use when writing Django backend code.

plugins.name: dev-dockerfile
plugins.description: Create production-grade Dockerfiles with multi-stage builds, security hardening, and optimal layer caching. Use when writing or improving Dockerfiles.

plugins.name: dev-event-driven
plugins.description: Design and implement event-driven architecture patterns with Kafka, RabbitMQ, or cloud-native messaging. Use when building async messaging, event sourcing, or pub/sub systems.

plugins.name: dev-flow
plugins.description: Complete structured development workflow — Explore, Plan, Implement (TDD), Review, Document. Use when developing any feature, fixing bugs, or making significant code changes. Replaces the old /epct command.

plugins.name: dev-flutter
plugins.description: Develop features in Flutter apps using Riverpod, clean architecture, and TDD. Use when writing Flutter/Dart code.

plugins.name: dev-go
plugins.description: Develop features in Go services using clean architecture, TDD, and idiomatic Go patterns. Use when writing Go backend code.

plugins.name: dev-helm
plugins.description: Create or modify Helm charts for Kubernetes deployments with proper templating, values, and best practices. Use when building or updating Helm charts.

plugins.name: dev-k8s-operator
plugins.description: Develop Kubernetes operator features with proper reconciliation patterns, CRD design, and testing. Use when writing K8s controller/operator code.

plugins.name: dev-maintain
plugins.description: Audit and improve code maintainability — SOLID principles, coupling analysis, complexity reduction, dependency health, and tech debt management. Use when assessing or improving code quality and maintainability.

plugins.name: dev-nextjs
plugins.description: Develop features in Next.js + React + TypeScript projects with App Router, Server Components, and testing. Use when writing Next.js frontend code.

plugins.name: dev-observability
plugins.description: Set up application observability with structured logging, metrics, and distributed tracing using OpenTelemetry. Use when adding monitoring, logging, or tracing to a service.

plugins.name: dev-plan
plugins.description: Create or update project plan documentation — module maps, architecture docs, dependency diagrams, and development roadmaps. Use when documenting or planning project architecture.

plugins.name: dev-springboot
plugins.description: Develop features in Spring Boot + Kotlin projects using TDD, clean architecture, and enterprise patterns. Use when writing Spring Boot backend code.

plugins.name: doc-adr
plugins.description: Create Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) documenting significant technical decisions with context, alternatives, and consequences. Use when making or documenting architectural choices.

plugins.name: doc-flow
plugins.description: Generate comprehensive internal documentation — project overview, architecture, dependency graphs, component interaction flows with Mermaid sequence diagrams, port boundary crossings, data type flows, and error propagation. Use when documenting how a project is structured and how components interact at runtime.

plugins.name: git-commit
plugins.description: Create well-structured git commits with conventional commit messages, proper staging, and pre-commit checks. Use when committing code changes.

plugins.name: git-pr
plugins.description: Create well-structured pull requests with proper title, description, and test plan using the gh CLI. Use when creating or updating pull requests.

plugins.name: init-django
plugins.description: Scaffold a new Django + DRF project with DDD structure, sub-apps, TDD, and production-ready config using uv. Use when creating a new Django/Python backend service.

plugins.name: init-fastapi
plugins.description: Scaffold a new FastAPI project with clean architecture, async patterns, and TDD using uv. Use when creating a new FastAPI backend service.

plugins.name: init-flutter
plugins.description: Scaffold a new Flutter mobile/web app with clean architecture and testing. Use when creating a new Flutter application.

plugins.name: init-go
plugins.description: Scaffold a new Go service with clean architecture, DDD, and TDD. Use when creating a new Go backend service or microservice.

plugins.name: init-k8s-operator
plugins.description: Scaffold a new Kubernetes operator using kubebuilder or operator-sdk. Use when creating a K8s custom controller or operator.

plugins.name: init-nextjs
plugins.description: Scaffold a new Next.js + React + TypeScript project with proper structure and testing. Use when creating a new frontend web application.

plugins.name: init-springboot
plugins.description: Scaffold a new Spring Boot + Kotlin project with DDD, TDD, and clean architecture. Use when creating a new Spring Boot backend service.

plugins.name: init-terraform
plugins.description: Scaffold Terraform infrastructure-as-code projects with proper module structure, state management, and environment separation. Use when creating or organizing Terraform IaC.

plugins.name: refactor-code
plugins.description: Systematically refactor code using proven patterns while maintaining behavior. Use when improving code structure, reducing complexity, or paying down tech debt.

plugins.name: review-code
plugins.description: Perform a thorough code review checking architecture, correctness, security, performance, testing, and code quality. Use when reviewing code changes or pull requests.

plugins.name: security-audit
plugins.description: Perform a security audit on code, checking for OWASP Top 10, injection, auth issues, secrets, and data exposure. Use when reviewing code for security vulnerabilities.

plugins.name: security-threat-model
plugins.description: Create a threat model for a system or feature using STRIDE methodology. Use when designing or reviewing system security architecture.

plugins.name: test-e2e
plugins.description: Write end-to-end tests for full user workflows using Playwright, Cypress, or API-level E2E tests. Use when testing complete user journeys.

plugins.name: test-generate
plugins.description: Generate comprehensive test cases and test suites for existing code. Analyzes code to produce unit, edge case, and error path tests. Use when you need tests for existing code.

plugins.name: test-integration
plugins.description: Write integration tests for APIs, databases, and external system boundaries using Testcontainers and real dependencies. Use when testing system boundaries.

plugins.name: test-performance
plugins.description: Write load and performance tests using k6 or Locust to validate throughput, latency, and reliability under load. Use when testing application performance.

plugins.name: test-unit
plugins.description: Write unit tests following TDD with proper patterns for any language. Use when you need to write or improve unit tests.

plugins.name: ux-audit
plugins.description: Perform a UX audit on a web/mobile application checking usability heuristics, accessibility, and user flow quality. Use when reviewing UI/UX of an application.
